frameworks.name: disentangled representation learning
frameworks.one_line: beta-VAE separates sensory data into interpretable latent generative factors

frameworks.name: deep supervised classification model of ventral stream
frameworks.one_line: distributed high-dimensional population code with uninterpretable single units

frameworks.name: single-unit semantic coding in IT
frameworks.one_line: single IT neurons map onto individual disentangled generative factors

core_question: Do single inferotemporal neurons encode interpretable generative factors of faces that align with an unsupervised disentangling objective?
key_assumptions: ventral stream may be optimising a disentangling objective
interpretable latent factors (e.g. gender, hair length) are recoverable by beta-VAE
macaque IT face responses are an appropriate substrate to test the alignment
manipulations: model class: deep unsupervised generative (beta-VAE) vs deep supervised classifier
face images as sensory input to both model and neural recordings
reconstruction from a small number of IT cell signals
key_findings: remarkable correspondence between beta-VAE generative factors and single IT neuron tuning
face images can be reconstructed using signals from just a handful of IT cells
interpretations: the ventral visual stream may be optimising a disentangling objective
the IT neural code may be low-dimensional and semantically interpretable at the single-unit level
